title: citrus grandis var. pyriformis
intro: Citrus grandis var. pyriformis, also known as the 'Pomelo', is a variety of the pummelo fruit tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, green-skinned fruit and sweet, juicy pulp. The medicinal properties of the Pomelo include its high vitamin C content, which can help boost the immune system, and its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The health benefits of consuming the Pomelo fruit include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving oral health, and supporting weight management. The herbal preparations of Citrus grandis var. pyriformis include the fresh fruit, which can be eaten raw or used in teas, as well as dried peel, which can be made into capsules or infusions for medicinal purposes.
